finding: A path-scoped `git commit -- <paths>` still runs ATM pre-commit against the entire staged index, so an operator cannot safely rescue or land a narrow fix when unrelated staged files already exist in the same worktree. This makes scoped recovery commits behave as though they were full-index commits and can block urgent evidence or hotfix backfills without touching other people's staged work.
expectedBehavior: ATM hook and governed git lanes should understand the effective commit surface for path-scoped commits. At minimum, the hook should distinguish the requested pathspec surface from unrelated staged entries and validate only the commit-bound file set, or provide a first-class isolated commit lane that preserves other staged state without requiring users to unstage foreign work.
evidenceOrRepro: Repaired on 2026-06-28 by keeping `GIT_INDEX_FILE` intact when ATM sanitizes git hook environments, so native path-scoped `git commit -- <paths>` continues to validate only the temporary pathspec index instead of collapsing back to the caller's full staged surface. Focused regression coverage in `scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts` now proves the commit lands only the requested pathspec while unrelated staged files remain staged and untouched in the real index.
followUp: Keep the pathspec regression in `scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts`, and treat any future hook env sanitation touching git index variables as release-critical because it can silently widen commit scope again.
